title: fatraea buxifolia
intro:
Fatraea buxifolia, also known as Boxwood Fatraea, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Apocynaceae, characterized by its small, white, bell-shaped flowers and fragrant leaves.

The medicinal properties of Fatraea buxifolia are primarily attributed to its essential oils, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. 

Its health benefits include its potential to reduce inflammation, relieve stress and anxiety, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions.

Fatraea buxifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
